meta.name: Belgium
meta.aboutStudying: Belgium sits at the heart of Europe and is home to some of the continent's oldest universities. Tuition is low (around €1,000/year for EU students) and the country offers an extraordinary number of English-taught Master's programmes. As the capital of the EU, Belgium is unrivalled for students of law, politics and international relations.

tips.title: Low EU Tuition
tips.text: EU students pay around €950/year at Belgian universities — among the lowest in Western Europe. Non-EU students pay €2,500–6,000/year. Living costs are moderate: €800–1,200/month including rent, cheaper than the Netherlands or France.

tips.title: Three Languages
tips.text: Belgium has Dutch-speaking Flanders (north), French-speaking Wallonia (south) and bilingual Brussels. Most Master's programmes are taught in English, but learning some Dutch or French will hugely enrich daily life and broaden your job prospects.

tips.title: Capital of Europe
tips.text: Brussels hosts the European Commission, Parliament and NATO. For students of law, politics, economics and international affairs, no city offers more internships and career opportunities. Belgium is also famed for chocolate, beer and waffles.

universities.name: KU Leuven
universities.desc: Belgium's highest-ranked university and one of the oldest in the world — a QS top-70 institution and Europe's most innovative university.

universities.name: Ghent University
universities.desc: A top-150 global university in one of Belgium's most beautiful and lively student cities.

universities.name: UCLouvain
universities.desc: The largest French-speaking university in Belgium, set in a purpose-built student town created entirely for the campus.

universities.name: Université libre de Bruxelles
universities.desc: A leading free-thinking research university in the European capital, with strong programmes in politics and law.

universities.name: Vrije Universiteit Brussel
universities.desc: A young, internationally minded Dutch-speaking university in the heart of Brussels with many English Master's programmes.

universities.name: University of Antwerp
universities.desc: A research university in Belgium's vibrant fashion and diamond capital, known for its medical and business faculties.

universities.name: University of Liège
universities.desc: The only complete public university in French-speaking Belgium, with a strong veterinary and engineering reputation.

universities.name: Hasselt University
universities.desc: A smaller, fast-rising university known for life sciences and a strong sense of community in eastern Flanders.

Brussels.desc: The capital of Europe — multilingual, cosmopolitan and unbeatable for students of law, politics and international affairs, with EU institutions on the doorstep.
Brussels.studentLife: Highly international — students from across the EU and the world.
Brussels.transport: Extensive metro, tram and bus network; high-speed trains to Paris, London, Amsterdam.
Brussels.nightlife: Diverse and cosmopolitan — from Belgian beer cafés to international clubs.

Leuven.desc: Belgium's ultimate student town — KU Leuven dominates this compact, beautiful and historic city where students make up a huge share of the population.
highlights: Old Market (longest bar in the world)
Leuven.studentLife: Among the best in Europe — the whole city revolves around the university.
Leuven.transport: 20 min by train to Brussels; very walkable and bike-friendly.
Leuven.nightlife: The Oude Markt is famously called 'the longest bar in the world'.

Ghent.desc: A stunning medieval canal city that locals call the best-kept secret in Europe — with a huge student population and one of the continent's most atmospheric old towns.
Ghent.studentLife: Exceptional — Ghent is one of Belgium's liveliest and most progressive cities.
Ghent.transport: Excellent trams and trains; 30 min to Brussels, 25 min to Bruges.
Ghent.nightlife: Buzzing — countless student bars and one of Belgium's best music scenes.

Antwerp.desc: Belgium's second city — the global diamond capital and a world fashion centre, blending a grand historic core with cutting-edge design and Europe's second-largest port.
Antwerp.studentLife: Stylish and creative — strong fashion, design and business student scene.
Antwerp.transport: Magnificent central station; trams, buses and 45 min to Brussels.
Antwerp.nightlife: Trendy and varied — from designer cocktail bars to harbour-side clubs.

cityCards.desc: The capital of Europe — multilingual, cosmopolitan and unbeatable for law, politics and international affairs.
Belgium's ultimate student town — KU Leuven dominates this beautiful and historic city.
A stunning medieval canal city with a huge student population — Europe's best-kept secret.
Belgium's second city — global diamond capital and a world fashion centre.
